_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the L8 'Footman' composited keeper and produce the L9 'Knight-Sergeant' variant (Tier 3 top).
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Knight-Sergeant beat = 5 visible adds (closed visor extending L8 half-visor downward to fully conceal the face, modest steel horns rising from the helm temples adding vertical silhouette extent, GOLD BAND wrapping the helm at the brim line, GOLD-TRIMMED shield rim band replacing the iron rim plus a small gold ring around the central boss dome, GOLD FILIGREE scrollwork on the upper face of each pauldron and centered on each tasset). FACE FULLY CONCEALED at L9 — no skin / hair / eyes / beard / mustache visible above the bevor. Within-tier chained input strategy. Re-emphasized gold language because L8 first-gold (pauldron seam rivets) landed only subtly — L9 gold needs to read prominently as buttery polished gold metal against cool silver-gray steel.

v2 (2026-05-07): revised per AD FeedbackV0 — REMOVED gold filigree scrollwork on pauldrons + tassets per AD 'rely on shapes/silhouettes, not sculpted details/filigree' — surface ornament that does not read at thumbnail. REMOVED gold helm circumference band as a NEW L9 add (it now arrives at L8 per the moved-up first-gold pass; L9 preserves the L8 helm band rather than adding it again). NEW L9 add: TASSETS GAIN OUTWARD FLARE at the lower edge (volume bump per Detail Economy / silhouette-mightiness rule, replacing the deprecated filigree pass). Closed visor + modest horns + gold shield rim + gold ring around boss carry over. Updated CRITICAL PRESERVATION: L8 first gold = helm trim band (not pauldron rivets); cuirass has no engraved scrollwork (removed at L8); tassets at L8 are 2 wider lames (not 3 narrow); full sabatons preserved from L8.
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.

v6 (2026-05-07): scrub stale L7 "~16 rim band studs" PRESERVE references — L7 add was rewritten to drop the rim-band stud accumulation per AD detail-economy; surgical pass also dropped remaining rim-stud language.

v6 (2026-05-07): drop the "studs become gold" clause from L9 shield add and the PRESERVE "+ ~16 rim studs" reference — L7 ~16 stud accumulation was removed, so the L9 gold rim band is now a clean continuous gold band only (no per-stud gold rivet heads).

v6 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27.

adds.title: CLOSED VISOR EXTENDING THE L8 HALF-VISOR DOWNWARD TO FULLY CONCEAL THE FACE
adds.summary: extend the existing half-visor downward with a steel lower-visor plate to fully close the face.

adds.title: MODEST UPWARD-CURVING STEEL HORNS RISING FROM THE HELM TEMPLES
adds.summary: add a pair of small upward-curving steel horns rising from the temple area on each side of the helm dome (above the ear position).

adds.title: BUTTERY GOLD SHIELD RIM BAND PLUS GOLD RING AROUND THE BOSS
adds.summary: REPLACE the iron rim band on the round shield (the dark-iron rim with ~16 studs from L7/L8) with a continuous BUTTERY POLISHED GOLD rim band wrapping the entire shield perimeter, approximately ½ inch wide, saturated buttery yellow-gold matching the helm band.

adds.title: TASSETS GAIN PRONOUNCED OUTWARD FLARE AT THE LOWER EDGE
adds.summary: exaggerate the outward flare at the lower edge of each tasset so each lame's bottom edge angles approximately 10 degrees outward, making the bottom corners visibly wider than the top corners.

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Infantry/out/v1/L8/composite/infantry_L8_v2_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the 'Footman' kit accumulated on top of the existing base. You MUST preserve every prior feature shown in the input image: (a) the STEEL SALLET HELM dome and forward-projecting brim with brim rivets AND the existing BUTTERY GOLD TRIM BAND wrapping the helm at the brim line (the existing first-gold pass — preserved at this level); the new step EXTENDS the existing half-visor downward to a fully-closed visor and ADDS horns, but the helm dome, brim, and existing gold band are all preserved; (b) the FULL STEEL CUIRASS — RAISED MEDIAL RIDGE plus ONE simple horizontal seam at the bottom-of-sternum line (NOT 3 articulated lames; engraved scrollwork was REMOVED at — the cuirass at has NO surface engraving, only the medial ridge and seam volume); preserved at this level; (c) the WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle visible between the tassets at the front; (d) the LONGER 2-LAME STEEL TASSETS extending to mid-thigh — exactly 2 WIDER lames per tasset (NOT 3 narrow lames) with slight outward flare at the lower edge from; the new step EXAGGERATES the outward flare at the lower edge per Edit Goal item 4 (the tassets gain a more pronounced outward flare — VOLUME bump replacing the deprecated filigree pass); NO gold filigree is added on the tassets at this level (filigree REMOVED 'rely on shapes/silhouettes, not sculpted details/filigree'); (e) the 3-LAME STEEL PAULDRONS on both shoulders (small upper disc + medium base disc + downward bicep lame) — preserved at in shape; NO gold rivet heads at the pauldron seam (the existing first-gold beat MOVED to a clear gold helm trim band; pauldron-seam rivets are NOT gold), NO gold filigree on the upper face of each pauldron (filigree REMOVED); (f) the STEEL VAMBRACES on both forearms extending continuously into STEEL GAUNTLETS at the wrists with knuckle-plates and articulated finger-plates; (g) the SOLID FLANGED STEEL MACE HEAD with 6 vertical flanges held in the gauntleted anatomical-RIGHT hand; (h) the WOODEN MACE HAFT with leather grip cord on the upper third and iron reinforcement strip running its length; (i) the LARGER STEEL-FACED ROUND SHIELD with the embossed sun-burst boss (raised central dome with 8 short triangular rays), the four iron cross strap-bands, and the iron rim band — the new step REPLACES the iron rim band with a GOLD rim band and ADDS a gold ring around the central boss dome, but the round shape, the cross strap-bands, the studs, and the sun-burst rays are all preserved; (j) the STEEL SABATONS over warm-brown leather boots (toe caps and ankle straps); (k) the LIVERY SKIRT BAND visible through the narrow ~2-inch front gap between the two tassets — the red+mustard split tunic skirt band remains visible at this level (livery transition to skirt-only band the levels above); (l) the BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH on anatomical-RIGHT hip and the BROWN LEATHER KNIFE SHEATH with cream bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip (visible at the upper edge of each tasset); (m) the MAIL COIF back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm. DO NOT revert any of these features. EDIT GOAL — Apply FOUR visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the input image, marking the top of Tier 3 (Knight-Sergeant). The face becomes FULLY CONCEALED for the first time (closed visor); the helm gains horns; the shield gains a gold rim band and a gold ring around the boss; the tassets gain pronounced outward flare at the lower edge (VOLUME bump replacing the deprecated pauldron+tasset filigree pass 'rely on shapes/silhouettes, not sculpted details/filigree'). Silhouette mightiness rule active: the new horns add vertical extent at the head silhouette and the flared tassets broaden the hip silhouette. The first-gold helm trim band is preserved at this level (it was the existing first-gold pass, not a new add). CRITICAL gold rendering note (read this carefully): every gold element at MUST render as BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL with a clearly distinct warm-yellow hue contrasting strongly against the cool silver-gray steel surfaces. The gold should be SATURATED and IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold at thumbnail scale — NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint, NOT mustard tint, NOT a warm-tinted highlight on steel. Render gold with: (i) a saturated buttery-yellow base tone (warm yellow-orange, like polished 18-karat gold, distinctly more yellow than the surrounding cool gray steel); (ii) a soft top-down highlight that reads almost white-yellow; (iii) a deeper warm shadow on the underside that reads honey-orange. Each gold element must be visually unmistakable as gold, not as a steel surface with warm lighting. (1) CLOSED VISOR EXTENDING THE HALF-VISOR DOWNWARD TO FULLY CONCEAL THE FACE — extend the existing half-visor downward with a steel lower-visor plate to fully close the face. The closed visor now covers the entire face from forehead down to the top of the bevor (where the bevor meets the breastplate at the throat). The visor has a single horizontal eye slit at eye level (approximately 1 inch tall, 4 inches wide, dark/shadowed inside) and a row of small breathing perforations (approximately 5-7 small dark circular holes) running horizontally across the lower-visor section roughly at mouth level. The visor is cool silver-gray polished steel matching the helm body. THE FACE IS NOW FULLY CONCEALED — no warm peachy-tan skin visible anywhere above the bevor; no eyes visible (only the dark slit); no nose tip visible; no mustache visible; no beard visible; no chin visible; no hair visible. The character's identity above the bevor is now entirely the helm + visor silhouette. The visor has a soft three-dimensional contour with a subtle medial ridge running vertically down the center (matching the cuirass medial ridge). (2) MODEST UPWARD-CURVING STEEL HORNS RISING FROM THE HELM TEMPLES — add a pair of small upward-curving steel horns rising from the temple area on each side of the helm dome (above the ear position). Each horn is approximately 6 inches tall, sweeps slightly OUTWARD and slightly BACK from the helm body (not straight up), pointed at the tip, cool silver-gray polished steel matching the helm. The horns are tapered (wider at the base where they attach to the helm, narrowing to a sharp point at the tip). The horn bases attach with a small rounded gold mount at each temple (small buttery-gold disc where the horn meets the helm — this is part of the existing gold language; the horn body itself is steel). The horns add visible vertical extent to the silhouette — the helm-plus-horns now reads taller than the existing helm alone (silhouette mightiness rule). (3) BUTTERY GOLD SHIELD RIM BAND PLUS GOLD RING AROUND THE BOSS — REPLACE the iron rim band on the round shield (clean wider band, no studs) with a continuous BUTTERY POLISHED GOLD rim band wrapping the entire shield perimeter, approximately ½ inch wide, saturated buttery yellow-gold matching the helm band. ADDITIONALLY add a thin GOLD RING around the central boss dome at the base of the boss where it meets the shield face — the gold ring is approximately ¼ inch thick, encircling the boss like a halo, distinctly buttery-gold against the cool silver-gray of the boss dome and the shield face. The boss dome itself remains cool silver-gray steel; the sun-burst rays remain cool silver-gray; the gold is ONLY at the boss's outer ring AND at the shield rim. The four iron cross strap-bands remain dark cool-gray iron (NOT gold at this level — only the rim and the boss-ring are gold on the shield). (4) TASSETS GAIN PRONOUNCED OUTWARD FLARE AT THE LOWER EDGE (VOLUME BUMP per silhouette-mightiness rule, replacing the deprecated pauldron+tasset gold filigree pass) — exaggerate the outward flare at the lower edge of each tasset so each lame's bottom edge angles approximately 10 degrees outward, making the bottom corners visibly wider than the top corners. The tassets remain 2 WIDER LAMES per tasset (NOT a return to 3 narrow lames — wider plates, fewer of them); the lames themselves do not multiply, only their bottom-edge silhouette flares more strongly outward. The hip silhouette is now visibly broader at the lower edge of the tassets than at this level — silhouette mightiness rule active without surface decoration. NO gold filigree, NO gold piping, NO scrollwork on the pauldrons or tassets at this level — the volume bump (the increased outward flare) carries the upgrade. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: the helm dome, brim, brim rivets; the cuirass with its 3 horizontal abdominal lames and the tonal scrollwork engraving on the medial ridge; the layered 3-disc/lame pauldrons on both shoulders (small upper disc + base disc + downward bicep lame) with the existing gold seam rivets remaining as-is; the steel vambraces extending into steel gauntlets with knuckle-plates and articulated finger-plates; the wide leather belt with square dark-iron buckle; the brown leather hip pouch on anatomical-RIGHT hip; the brown leather knife sheath with bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip; the longer 3-lame steel tassets with outward flare; the LIVERY skirt band visible through the front tasset gap; the dark blue / muted steel-blue trousers visible at the calf; the steel sabatons over warm-brown leather boots; the leather grip cord on the upper haft; the iron reinforcement strip on the wooden haft; the wooden mace haft (the haft remains wood; the mace head is the existing flanged steel head); the flanged steel mace head with 6 vertical flanges; the larger steel-faced round shield with the central steel boss + sun-burst rays + four iron cross strap-bands; the mail coif back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE MACE ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, gauntleted hand grips the upper haft, flanged steel mace head dangling at hip height angled down. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) bent at elbow, round shield up at upper-chest / shoulder height. Calm guard stance. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Match the framing of the input image as closely as possible (vfill ~70%, slight tier-break growth from L1_Base envelope). The new horns on the helm may extend the silhouette UPWARD slightly — that small vertical growth is acceptable. DO NOT zoom in further, DO NOT crop the horns at the top of the canvas, DO NOT shift the figure downward. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT leave the face open at this level — the visor MUST be FULLY CLOSED; no skin, no hair, no eyes, no nose, no mustache, no beard visible above the bevor; DO NOT show ANY warm peachy-tan skin tone above the bevor — the entire upper face must be steel; DO NOT make the visor a half-visor — extend it downward to cover the entire face from forehead to bevor; DO NOT make the eye slit large or open — single narrow horizontal slit; DO NOT add multiple eye slits or vertical slits; DO NOT make the breathing perforations a face grille or honeycomb — single horizontal row of ~5-7 small dark circular holes only; DO NOT make the horns large dragon-style or curling ram-style horns — they are MODEST, upward-curving, sweeping slightly outward and back, ~6 inches tall (large royal horns + central crest arrive at this level); DO NOT add a central crest between the horns at this level — that arrives at this level; DO NOT make the horns gold or gilt — the horn body is steel; only the small mount disc at each horn base is gold; DO NOT replace the entire helm body with gold — gold at is ONLY the existing helm trim band (preserved), the horn-mount discs (small gold mounts at the horn bases), the shield rim band, and the gold ring around the boss; DO NOT add gold filigree / scrollwork / etched motifs on the pauldrons or tassets at this level — filigree was REMOVED 2026-05-07 ('rely on shapes/silhouettes, not sculpted details/filigree'); the existing hip / shoulder upgrades are SILHOUETTE-only (tasset flare); DO NOT add per-edge gold piping on the cuirass or tassets at this level (per-edge piping arrives at was REMOVED — see prompt); DO NOT make the gold dim, brass-toned, bronze-toned, or yellow-painted — gold MUST render as buttery polished saturated YELLOW GOLD METAL with strong contrast against the cool silver-gray steel; DO NOT use mustard or olive yellow for gold — gold is buttery WARM YELLOW; DO NOT make the cuirass medial ridge engraving gold — it remains tonal-only at this level (gold cuirass piping arrives at this level); DO NOT add a gold cross emblem to the shield face — that arrives at this level; DO NOT add gold knuckle-plates on the gauntlets — those arrive at this level; DO NOT add a fourth lame to the pauldrons — they remain 3-lame at this level (4-lame with sculpted lion's-head motif arrives at this level); DO NOT extend the tassets longer than at this level; DO NOT change the round shield to any non-round shape; DO NOT change the boss color to gold — the boss DOME remains cool silver-gray steel (only the gold ring AROUND the boss is added at this level); DO NOT replace the wooden mace haft with metal; DO NOT change the mace head shape; DO NOT make the mace head gold or partially gold — gold mace arrives at this level; DO NOT shrink the livery skirt band to nothing — it remains visible through the front tasset gap; DO NOT change the trouser color or boot color (boots remain warm-brown leather where the sabatons do not cover); DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT swap the hand holding the mace; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure (especially do not crop the new horns at the top); DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
